title: The Fractured Post-American World and the Future of Democracy
content:
Like all heroic achievements, the historical concept of Pax Americana carries its own myths, elisions, and exaggerations. While Washington sometimes secured a liberal order, it occasionally relied on illiberal expedients such as brutal military interventions and covert intrigues. Furthermore, romanticized odes to allied solidarity often neglect the vitriolic disputes that periodically rocked the democratic world. The United States also flouted or changed its own international rules when they became inconvenient, proving that no geopolitical order exists without some hypocrisy and coercion. Despite these significant flaws, this period of American leadership undeniably facilitated remarkable global success and stability.

However, the old world order is currently dying, partly due to states systematically destroying it from within. For instance, Beijing utilized its insertion into the global economy to build the manufacturing and military heft it now uses to challenge the United States. Meanwhile, Washington itself has become increasingly fatigued and fatally disillusioned with the very international system it helped create. This growing ambivalence stems from very real problems, including persistent financial imbalances, free-riding in U.S. alliances, and the enormous economic costs of foreign interventions. Consequently, the world is now witnessing a dramatic shift towards a more fragmented, post-American crossroads.

Looking ahead, the potential scenarios for the future global order are significantly uglier and far less stable. The world might witness a new age of empires or a devastating descent back into chaos, paths that tempt superpowers who have forgotten the horrors preceding Pax Americana. The irony is that the United States still possesses an outsized influence on what follows the very order it is abandoning. Depending on leadership choices, America could either rebuild the free world for a new era or accidentally trigger a disastrous neoisolationist movement. Ultimately, these critical decisions will shape the global balance of power for generations to come.
